varieties.name: Viola banksii (Native Violet) 🌏 INDIGENOUS
varieties.origin: Native to east coast Australia, near Brisbane to Batemans Bay, named after Joseph Banks who collected specimens at Botany Bay in 1770
varieties.traits: Prostrate groundcover, spreading by layering stems, kidney-shaped bright green leaves 15-25mm diameter
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific flowering, can spread to cover very large areas in moist positions
varieties.growing_notes: Exceptionally well-adapted to Brisbane subtropical climate. Hardy in range of conditions. Prefers moist, sunny to semi-shaded locations. Tolerates Brisbane heat better than exotic species. Ideal as groundcover, in containers, or hanging baskets. Formerly known as V. hederacea.
varieties.availability: Widely available: Australian Plants Online, Sunshine Coast Plants, Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery, The Plant Hub (ships to Brisbane), Plantmark Wholesale Nursery

varieties.name: Viola hederacea (Native Violet) 🌏 INDIGENOUS
varieties.origin: Tasmania and southern regions (true V. hederacea, as revised by Kevin Thiele 2003-2004)
varieties.traits: Low-growing evergreen, circular heart-shaped leaves, forms dense mat, 5-15cm height, spreading to 2m width
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for Brisbane with adequate moisture. Prefers partial to full shade. Adaptable to various soil types. Excellent for shady spots, between pavers, lawn alternative in shade. Fast-spreading groundcover. Requires regular water in hot, dry weather.
varieties.availability: Queensland nurseries, Sunshine Coast Plants (delivers Brisbane), Daleys Fruit Tree

varieties.name: Swiss Giants Mix 🏆 HEIRLOOM
varieties.origin: Developed 1800s, crossing Viola lutea, V. tricolor, and V. altaica. Named for Swedish botanist Veit Brecher Wittrock
varieties.traits: Large flowers 6-8cm (2.5-3.5 inches), compact growth 15-20cm tall, mounding habit

varieties.name: Johnny Jump Up (Viola tricolor) 🏆 HEIRLOOM
varieties.origin: European wildflower (heartsease), naturalized worldwide. Progenitor of modern pansies
varieties.traits: Compact 10-15cm tall, spreading habit, self-sows freely. Smaller than modern pansies

varieties.name: Victorian Posy 🏆 HEIRLOOM
varieties.origin: Renee's Garden exclusive, bred to resemble Victorian-era pansies (1870-1900)
varieties.traits: Taller than modern pansies, ruffled velvety petals, romantic appearance
varieties.growing_notes: Nostalgic Victorian-style flowers. Best for Brisbane winter season. Plant autumn for winter-spring blooms. Excellent for containers and borders. Heat-sensitive.

varieties.name: Matrix Series 🧬 HYBRID
varieties.origin: Modern breeding, designed for extreme uniformity and heat tolerance
varieties.traits: Compact habit, strong short stems hold flowers above foliage, 15-20cm tall
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT FOR BRISBANE. Among the most heat-tolerant pansies available. Outstanding performance in subtropical conditions. Rebounds well from extreme weather. Suitable for Brisbane autumn-spring planting with extended season. Modern breeding provides disease resistance.
varieties.availability: Commercial seed suppliers, Sedan Floral, widespread availability

varieties.name: Cool Wave Series 🧬 HYBRID
varieties.origin: PanAmerican Seed (makers of Wave petunias), modern trailing pansy breakthrough
varieties.traits: Trailing spreading habit, cascades 60-75cm (24-30 inches), vigorous growth
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific spreading coverage, fewer plants needed per pot
varieties.growing_notes: Most cold-hardy pansy (hardy to zone 5). Excellent for Brisbane hanging baskets and containers. Trailing habit ideal for vertical displays. Good performance autumn-spring in Brisbane. Heat will limit summer survival.
varieties.availability: Harris Seeds, Ball Seed, True Leaf Market, major suppliers

varieties.name: Majestic Giants II 🧬 HYBRID
varieties.traits: Very large blooms up to 10cm, strong growth, bushy mounding habit
varieties.growing_notes: Popular for Brisbane winter gardens. Plant autumn for winter-spring display. Excellent for borders, containers. Declines in summer heat. Edible flowers for garnishes.

varieties.name: Delta Series 🧬 HYBRID
varieties.origin: Modern breeding for early flowering and cold tolerance
varieties.traits: Uniform habit, compact plants, early blooming
varieties.growing_notes: Bred for unpredictable spring weather, suitable for Brisbane winter season. Good performance in cool conditions.

varieties.name: Viola serpens (syn. Viola pilosa) 🌏 INDIGENOUS
varieties.origin: Native to Himalayan region of India, Pakistan, Nepal, China, also found in Java, Philippines, Thailand, Ceylon
varieties.traits: Herbaceous perennial, prostrate stems to 100cm rooting at nodes, rosette of leaves. Variable species
varieties.growing_notes: COOLER ELEVATIONS ONLY in Kerala. Traditional medicinal plant. Prefers shady, moist environments. Grows wild in hill regions at 1500-2000m altitude. Not suitable for Kerala lowland tropical heat.
varieties.availability: Wild collection from hills, specialized medicinal plant nurseries in India, conservation status: declining due to overexploitation

varieties.name: Viola canescens (Himalayan White Violet) 🌏 INDIGENOUS
varieties.origin: Himalayan regions of Pakistan, India, Bhutan, Nepal. Also called Banafsha
varieties.traits: Perennial herb, subglabrous or hairy, almost prostrate. Prefers shady, moist places
varieties.growing_notes: COOLER ELEVATIONS ONLY. Cold desert and Himalayan species, requires cool temperatures. Endangered status due to overexploitation. NOT suitable for Kerala tropical lowlands. Potentially in Kerala hill stations above 1500m.
varieties.availability: Conservation concern - endangered. Specialized Indian botanical gardens, medicinal plant nurseries

varieties.name: Viola odorata (Sweet Violet) 🌱 OPEN-POLLINATED
varieties.origin: Native to Europe, Western Asia, naturalized in parts of India at higher altitudes (1500-1800m)
varieties.traits: Herbaceous perennial, stoloniferous (spreads by runners), heart-shaped leaves 6cm long, 10-15cm height

varieties.name: Viola betonicifolia 🌱 OPEN-POLLINATED
varieties.origin: Wide distribution: Afghanistan to Japan, including India (Assam, West Himalaya), Southeast Asia, Australia (Queensland, NSW)
varieties.traits: Perennial, grows primarily in subtropical biome
varieties.growing_notes: Wide distribution including subtropical and tropical regions. Found in Queensland, Australia and Indian subcontinent. Most suitable Viola species for lower elevations. Research needed for specific Kerala performance.
varieties.availability: Botanical collections, may be available from tropical plant specialists

varieties.name: Viola tricolor (Heartsease, Wild Pansy) 🏆 HEIRLOOM
varieties.origin: European wildflower, cultivated in India as ornamental, occasionally escapes
varieties.growing_notes: Cultivated as ornamental in Indian gardens but not well-adapted to Kerala tropical heat. May survive in cooler microclimates. Flowering November-May in India.
varieties.availability: Indian seed companies for ornamental use, limited availability
